title: Fix duplicate flow ids: nextFlowId races on concurrent init and resolveFlowDir silently picks one of the colliding flows

AC1.note: src/flow/allocation.test.ts: worktree-uniqueness + concurrent-init tests; ids allocated via git-common-dir lock+ledger

AC2.note: src/flow/allocation.test.ts 'outside a git checkout' test; resolveAllocationScope falls back to local flows listing

AC3.note: src/flow/duplicate-ids.test.ts ambiguous-id + exact/slug-resolve tests; resolveFlowDir throws naming candidates

AC4.note: duplicate-ids.test.ts: service.get/start reject; harness ManagedFlowPort fails closed and writes nothing

AC5.note: duplicate-ids.test.ts flow-check + duplicateFlowIds list-marker tests; kind:duplicate-id hard fail

AC6.note: duplicate-ids.test.ts renumber tests; flow renumber moves dir, rewrites flow.json id, renumbered history event, id-map.json; refuses taken/malformed/already-used/empty-reason

AC7.note: repaired 002->117, 084->118, 103->119 via renumber; id-map.json committed; keryx flow check reports zero duplicate-id issues

AC8.note: 002-gdgraph AC checksum mismatch left untouched (pre-existing on main); documented in flow 116 journal; no acceptance-criteria.md rewritten by renumber

AC9.note: keryx health run PASS (score 93); bun test 2111 pass/0 fail; new tests use OS tmpdirs (security-gate fixture moved out of repo)

AC10.note: .metaproject/skills/flow/SKILL.md 'Flow ids' section + memory constraint flow-ids-allocated-per-clone.md (accepted, indexed)

tasks.title: Add/adjust tests and make them pass

keryx flow renumber command (move dir, rewrite flow.json id, history event, old->new mapping)
Repair existing collisions 002/084/103 via renumber; handle the 002 AC checksum mismatch explicitly
Document repo-wide allocation + worktree rule in flow skill and project memory
